purpose: Paired-probe manifest for the H8 narrative-immersion hypothesis. Declares which narrative scenario form pairs with which abstract (quick-fire) form for a single construct, so the analyzer can compute the within-person divergence D_i^p = r_narr - r_abs (scoring.md §9.1). Mirrors inventory/pairwise-pairs.json: the pairing relationship lives in a manifest separate from the scenarios themselves. This file is the data contract; the scenarios it references are editorial work authored separately.

design_refs.scoring: scoring.md §9.1 (D divergence), §9.2 (H8a, low-stakes pool), §9.4 (H8b, high-stakes attachment-laden pool)
design_refs.decisions: DECISIONS.md §17 (these probes are the construct-gap that unlocks the §16 corpus 48 -> ~56-60)

entry_field_spec.pair_id: Stable unique id, pattern pp-{domain-slug}-{nnn} (pp = paired-probe).
entry_field_spec.construct: One-sentence description of the single construct probed in BOTH forms. The forms must be structurally equivalent — same choice, different framing (h8-narrative-immersion-design.md Q3).
entry_field_spec.domain: One of the four MVP-1 domains.
entry_field_spec.stakes_level: 'low' routes the pair into the H8a debiasing pool (scoring.md §9.2); 'high' routes it into the H8b attachment-laden pool (§9.4). Drives which test the pair contributes to.
entry_field_spec.narrative_ref: Which scenario+signal yields r_narr. {scenario_id, signal}. 'signal' names the scene/terminal/decision within the narrative whose primary-axis score is taken as the narrative-form response. HOW a narrative resolves to one scalar (terminal-based vs path-based) is the open question in scoring.md §11 and must be resolved before lock — this manifest only PINS which signal is read, not how it's scored.
entry_field_spec.abstract_ref: Which quick-fire item yields r_abs. {scenario_id, item_id}. The structurally-equivalent abstract form.
entry_field_spec.npc_ref: Required when stakes_level='high': the npc-cast.json id of the character whose welfare is at stake (so §9.4 can fetch that participant's attachment_strength for this character). Null for low-stakes pairs.
entry_field_spec.counterbalance: Always true in practice: presentation order (narrative-first vs abstract-first) is randomized across participants and entered as a covariate (scoring.md §9.5). Recorded per-entry only if a pair intentionally fixes order.

status: 9 pairs authored (toward the ~8-12 DECISIONS §17 target): truth-telling ×4 (H8a), resource-allocation ×1 (H8b), in-group-out-group ×2 (1 H8a + 1 H8b), reciprocity-cooperation ×2 (H8a). All four domains covered; 4 of 5 NPCs exercised; 2 high-stakes attachment probes (biscuit, gran). Authored via adversarially-verified workflows; all narratives + twins schema-valid. Remaining: optionally 1-3 more toward 12, plus the attachment-measurement instrument.

pairs.construct: Telling a close friend to their face that something they made did not land well with you, when a warm little lie would cost nothing and spare the moment.
Owning a small mistake of yours that quietly landed blame on someone you have a wary, complicated history with, when you could let it pass unnoticed and untraced.
Volunteering an unflattering truth about yourself to a close friend who has not asked and would never find out otherwise.
Whether to tell an aging family member a gentle but real truth about a capacity they are visibly losing, when reassurance would be kinder in the moment and easier for you.

Choosing, under irreversible time pressure, between the welfare of one dependent you have come to love and a larger but faceless claim of strangers you will never meet, where the resource (one trip, the same minutes, one dose) is genuinely indivisible so the two cannot both be served.

Whether to extend a real, recurring, costly inclusion to a newcomer who has no claim on you yet — folding them permanently into a small, settled circle you value precisely for being small — when keeping the clear, smaller circle would be easier and no one would fault you for it.

Whether to re-extend trust to someone who once defected on you and now shows genuine, costly signs of repair — risking being burned twice versus foreclosing a real reconciliation — when the new arrangement recreates the exact structure of the original betrayal.

Whether to keep quietly carrying more than your half of an ongoing, unspoken mutual arrangement with a close friend, or to name the imbalance and ask to rebalance it, at the risk of the frictionless reciprocity you both rely on.

Choosing, when the two cannot both be honored, between a costly and particular obligation to an aging family member who counts on you and a larger or fairer claim from outside your circle, where the resource is a single indivisible place so the two cannot both be served.
